items.body: North Berwick in July is links golf at its best — wind off the Firth, firm fescue, and a field that feels like a major dress rehearsal. Scheffler tops the board, McIlroy loves this stretch of coast, and Gotterup defends after winning in Silvis on Sunday. This is the final tune-up before the year's third major: the British Open at Royal Birkdale.

Someone's going to blame the wind, someone's going to blame the gorse, and someone's definitely going to three-putt from inside 1.82 meters. Betting favorite has to be Bobby Mac. He's on a first-name basis with all the best North Berwick hookers.

items.label: Scottie Scheffler (+445 to +550):
items.body: World No. 1 and clear betting favorite, coming off a Travelers playoff loss and hunting his second win of 2026.

items.label: Rory McIlroy (+850 to +1000):
items.body: Won the Scottish Open at Renaissance in 2023 and finished runner-up to Gotterup here last year.

items.label: Jon Rahm (+1100 to +1300):
items.body: Major champion back in the co-sanctioned field with the links game to contend on a firm coastal setup.

items.label: Xander Schauffele (+1800 to +2200):
items.body: Won this event in 2022 at Renaissance and keeps posting strong course history on Scotland's coast.

items.label: Ludvig Aberg (+2000 to +2600):
items.body: Top-tier ball-striker with the length and iron play to handle firm Renaissance fairways.

items.label: Tommy Fleetwood (+2000 to +2500):
items.body: A crowd favorite on links with the creativity to score when coastal wind kicks up.

items.label: Matt Fitzpatrick (+1900 to +2200):
items.body: Elite links form and one of the most reliable wind players in the top half of the board.

items.label: Chris Gotterup (+2600 to +3000):
items.body: Defending Scottish Open champion, riding confidence after closing 62 to win the John Deere on Sunday.

items.label: Viktor Hovland (+2800 to +3600):
items.body: Recent Travelers playoff winner with the ball-striking to contend on a fast, firm links setup.

items.label: Robert MacIntyre (+3000 to +3800):
items.body: 2024 Scottish Open winner at Renaissance — the hometown hero and first Scot to win here in a generation.

items.label: Venue:
items.body: The Renaissance Club in North Berwick, East Lothian, Scotland, along the Firth of Forth on Scotland's Golf Coast.

items.label: Established:
items.body: The Scottish Open dates to 1972 and has been co-sanctioned by the PGA Tour and DP World Tour since 2022; The Renaissance Club has hosted since 2019.

items.label: Defending Champion:
items.body: Chris Gotterup won the 2025 edition at 15-under, his second career PGA Tour title.

items.label: Tradition:
items.body: Scotland's national open and a DP World Tour Rolex Series event; the top three non-exempt finishers earn spots in The Open Championship.

items.label: Course:
items.body: The Renaissance Club, North Berwick, Scotland.

items.label: Dates:
items.body: July 9 through July 12, 2026.

items.label: Purse:
items.body: $9 million.

items.label: Format:
items.body: 156-player field (PGA Tour and DP World Tour), 72-hole stroke play.

items.label: Course Profile:
items.body: A Tom Doak-designed 7,282-yard par-70 links course with firm fescue fairways, pot bunkers, and coastal winds that reward creative shot-making.

items.label: Coverage:
items.body: Thursday and Friday on Golf Channel (11 a.m.-2 p.m. ET); weekend rounds on Golf Channel (10 a.m.-noon ET) and CBS (noon-3 p.m. ET), with streaming on Paramount+ and PGA TOUR LIVE on ESPN+.

items.label: Event Window:
items.body: Competition runs July 9 through July 12, 2026.
